message:
feat(core): upgrade capture relay from SPSC to SPMC broadcast channel

The lock-free ring buffer infrastructure has been upgraded from a
single-producer/single-consumer (SPSC) channel to a single-producer/
multi-consumer (SPMC) broadcast channel for the capture event hot path.

This change enables future addition of parallel lock-free consumers
(e.g. real-time analysis, metrics aggregation) on the capture event
stream without changing producer semantics or adding synchronization
overhead on the existing persistence consumer.

Concrete changes in spsc_ring_buffer.rs:
- Add spmc_channel() constructor that creates one producer and N
  consumer queues, each backed by its own crossbeam ArrayQueue
- Add SpmcShared<T> inner state with per-consumer queue, Notify pairs
- Add SpmcProducer<T> with send/try_send that broadcasts to all
  consumer queues atomically — send waits for the slowest consumer
- Add SpmcConsumer<T> with recv/try_recv, per-consumer depth and
  capacity inspection, and consumer_index() accessor
- Producer close/drop notifies all consumer waiters for clean shutdown
- Preserve existing SPSC channel() API fully unchanged
- Add comprehensive tests: broadcast ordering, backpressure from slow
  consumers, try_send failure when any consumer queue is full,
  close-then-drain semantics, zero-capacity and zero-consumer panics

Concrete changes in runtime.rs:
- Replace SpscConsumer/SpscProducer imports with SpmcConsumer/SpmcProducer
- Replace spsc channel() call with spmc_channel(buffer_size, 1) in
  ObservationRuntime pipeline setup, popping the single consumer
- Update spawn_capture_relay_task signature to accept SpmcProducer
- Update spawn_persistence_task signature to accept SpmcConsumer
- Update doc comments to reflect SPMC terminology

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
